assessment_questions.question_text: Our family has clearly named and written down our core values (for example, kindness, faith, honesty, or service).
We regularly talk with our children about why our family values matter and how they shape our choices.
Our daily and weekly routines (mealtimes, screen use, activities, rest) reflect the values we say are important.
When we set rules or boundaries, we explain them in terms of the kind of family we want to be, not just in terms of convenience.
We handle conflict in ways that model our values (for example, listening, apologizing, forgiving, and seeking restoration).
Our use of technology and social media (for both adults and kids) is guided more by our values than by habit or peer pressure.
We make room in our schedule for unhurried, meaningful time together each week (such as a family night, shared meal, or faith practice).
Our children would be able to explain at least two or three of our family values in their own words.
We talk openly about mistakes and use them as chances to learn and grow, rather than to shame or withdraw from each other.
Our spending and giving habits reflect what we say is important to us as a family (for example, generosity, simplicity, or hospitality).
We intentionally celebrate growth in character (such as honesty, courage, or self-control) at least as much as we celebrate achievements.
We invite our children to give input on how our family can better live out our values at home, at school, and in the community.

petitions.title: Support Parental Rights in Education in Texas
petitions.short_description: Urge Texas lawmakers and school districts to uphold parental rights in education, ensuring families are informed and involved in key decisions affecting their children.
petitions.full_text:
Parents are the primary caregivers and decision-makers in their children’s lives. Schools and families work best together when communication is clear, transparent, and rooted in mutual respect.

This petition calls on the Texas Legislature, the Texas Education Agency, and local school districts to:

1. Affirm Parental Rights in Education by clearly stating that parents have the right to access instructional materials, review counseling and support services offered to their children, and be notified about significant changes in their child’s well-being or educational plan.
2. Strengthen communication requirements so that schools provide timely, accessible information about curriculum, technology use, and student support services.
3. Develop clear, family-centered policies that honor diverse family values while maintaining a safe and supportive learning environment for every student.

We believe policies should empower parents as partners, not replace them. By signing this petition, you are asking Texas leaders to protect and promote parental rights in education while fostering constructive collaboration between families and schools.
petitions.target_entity: Texas State Legislature and Texas Education Agency

petitions.title: Advance Parental Rights in Education Nationwide
petitions.short_description: Call on national education leaders to recognize and protect parental rights in education policy across all states.
petitions.full_text:
Families across the country want schools to be strong partners in their children’s education. For that to happen, parental rights in education must be clearly respected and upheld in both law and practice.

This petition asks members of Congress, the U.S. Department of Education, and national education organizations to:

1. Affirm the vital role of parents as the first and primary educators of their children.
2. Encourage states to adopt transparent policies that give parents access to curriculum materials, student records, and information about support services.
3. Promote best practices for school–family communication that respect diverse family values while prioritizing student well-being.

By signing, you join families from many backgrounds who believe that healthy collaboration between schools and parents is essential for children to thrive.
petitions.target_entity: U.S. Congress and U.S. Department of Education

petitions.title: Promote Social Media Safety Education in Local Schools
petitions.short_description: Ask your local school board to adopt research-based social media safety education that partners with parents.
petitions.full_text:
Social media can connect students in positive ways, but it also brings risks such as cyberbullying, anxiety, and exposure to harmful content. Families need schools to be allies in teaching wise, values-based use of technology.

This petition urges local school boards and district leaders to:

1. Implement age-appropriate digital citizenship and social media safety curricula that involve families.
2. Offer regular parent workshops on social media trends, privacy, and boundaries.
3. Establish clear policies that address cyberbullying and misuse of technology while promoting restorative practices.

By signing, you support a balanced approach that equips students and parents to navigate social media safely and responsibly.
petitions.target_entity: Local School Boards and District Leaders

petitions.title: Set Healthy Screen Time Guidelines for School-Issued Devices
petitions.short_description: Encourage your district to create balanced, research-informed guidelines for student use of school-issued devices.
petitions.full_text:
The rapid expansion of 1:1 device programs has changed the way students learn. While technology can enrich education, families are concerned about excessive screen time and its impact on attention, sleep, and relationships.

This petition asks school district leaders to:

1. Establish clear limits on non-instructional screen use during the school day.
2. Coordinate assignments and online platforms to avoid unnecessary screen time at home.
3. Provide families with simple tools and guidance to manage school-related technology use.

When schools and parents work together, students can benefit from digital tools without sacrificing their well-being or family connections.

petitions.title: Protect Funding for Community-Based Marriage Support Programs
petitions.short_description: Ask policymakers to maintain and expand funding for nonprofit marriage education and support programs that strengthen families.
petitions.full_text:
Research shows that strong, healthy marriages benefit children, neighborhoods, and communities. Yet many couples lack access to affordable, values-centered support when they face stress, conflict, or major transitions.

This petition urges state and local policymakers to:

1. Protect existing grants for community-based marriage education and counseling programs.
2. Prioritize funding for organizations that serve low- and middle-income families.
3. Encourage partnerships between faith-based and community organizations to reach diverse couples.

By signing, you affirm the importance of accessible marriage support programs as a key part of a comprehensive family-strengthening strategy.

petitions.title: Expand Access to Sliding-Scale Family Counseling Services
petitions.short_description: Encourage community leaders to invest in sliding-scale family counseling so more families can get help when they need it.
petitions.full_text:
Many families want counseling support but are unable to afford private therapy or lack insurance coverage. Sliding-scale, nonprofit counseling centers bridge this gap and help families address conflict, grief, and mental health challenges.

This petition asks city and county leaders to:

1. Increase funding for nonprofit counseling centers that provide sliding-scale services.
2. Support outreach efforts so that families know where to find help.
3. Encourage collaboration between schools, faith communities, and counseling providers.

Together, we can make sure that cost is not a barrier for families seeking support and healing.

petitions.title: Parental Rights in Education – Early Awareness Campaign (Archived)
petitions.short_description: An earlier campaign encouraging schools to clearly communicate with parents about curriculum and student support options.
petitions.full_text:
This archived petition was part of an early awareness effort around parental rights in education. Thousands of families asked their districts to improve communication about curriculum choices, counseling services, and technology use.

The petition is now archived because its primary goals have been incorporated into ongoing advocacy efforts and updated resources. You can still read the text to understand the history of this campaign and how it helped shape current initiatives.

petitions.title: Establish Clear Cyberbullying Policies in Middle Schools
petitions.short_description: A completed campaign that encouraged districts to adopt clear, compassionate policies for addressing cyberbullying.
petitions.full_text:
Cyberbullying can deeply wound students and families. This petition asked districts to establish clear definitions, reporting procedures, and restorative responses that involve parents and prioritize student safety.

The active phase of this campaign has ended, and many districts have updated their policies as a result. Although this petition is now closed to new signatures, the resources it generated continue to inform our advocacy.

petition_signatures.personal_message: As a Texas parent, I want schools to communicate openly with families, respect our role in guiding our children, and partner with us on curriculum, safety, mental health, and support.
Please strengthen policies that keep parents informed and engaged while ensuring every student feels safe and supported at school.
Families and schools work best as partners; national policy should reflect that reality.
Our kids need guidance, not fear, around social media. Schools and parents must teach wise, safe use together.
Balanced guidelines for school devices will help protect family time and student focus.
Sliding-scale family counseling made a huge difference for us; every community should have this support available.
